title: Mexico Multiple Immigration Form (FMM) — Electronic, Land Entry
description: Mexico's Forma Migratoria Múltiple (FMM), the mandatory entry/exit record every visitor without permanent-resident status must present on arrival for tourism, business, or other short-term purposes, completed in advance through the National Immigration Institute's (Instituto Nacional de Migración, INM) own live, unauthenticated online wizard. Filling out the wizard produces a folio/record the traveler pays a separate fee against (via a distinct INM payment portal) and presents, together with their travel document, at the port of entry; this document models only the online data-entry wizard itself — not the fee payment, not the physical inspection at the border, and not the PDF the wizard can generate once a folio is confirmed. The live wizard offers only one reachable entry mode: this document is scoped to a land ('Terrestre'/'By land') entry, which is the only means-of-entry option this specific wizard's own client-side script allows a user to reach (see VERIFICATION.md); it also covers the ordinary adult pathway plus the conditional parent/guardian information block required whenever the applicant is a minor. The wizard's own mandatory CAPTCHA/verification-code step, which gates the final save action, is out of scope — this document never submits the form. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Mexico or the Instituto Nacional de Migración.

source.documentRef: Forma Migratoria Múltiple (FMM) — electronic online wizard, Instituto Nacional de Migración

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema opening Mexico as the registry's 16th jurisdiction. Authored against the live, unauthenticated INM FMM wizard (both the English UI at .../en/solicitud.html and the Spanish default at .../publico/solicitud.html — note the Spanish page has no /es/ path segment, unlike English/Japanese/Chinese/Korean, which do; .../publico/es/solicitud.html 404s) via headless-browser DOM extraction plus direct inspection of the wizard's own client-side validation source (jquery.inm.fmme.solicitud-1.0.0.js and jquery.inm.common.validator-3.1.4.js, both served unminified from inm.gob.mx) and its field-option catalog endpoint (/fmme/resources/solicitud/meta-resources/catalogos, an XML document despite its JSON-like path segment). Reading the validator source directly — not just the rendered DOM — was necessary because this wizard's required/hidden/pattern rules are applied entirely by a custom JS validation object, not native HTML `required`/`pattern` attributes (every native attribute on every field is unset). A full end-to-end dry run (fill every field with fabricated data, trigger every confirmation-mismatch case, trigger the minor/guardian branch, reach the client-side-valid review state) was completed without ever submitting past the CAPTCHA gate. Status remains 'draft' pending an independent second reviewer's field-by-field pass. See VERIFICATION.md for the extraction method, the land-only entry-mode scope decision, and other interpretive judgment calls flagged for that review.

fields.name: meansOfEntry
fields.label: Means of entry
fields.description: How the applicant will enter Mexico. This document models only the land ('Terrestre') pathway: on the live wizard this field is pre-selected to 'By land' and its native HTML `<select>` is disabled the moment the applicant accepts the terms checkbox under the page's single 'By Land' accordion panel — the wizard's own client-side script (`viaInternacion = "terrestre"`, hardcoded) never lets a user reach the alternative 'By air' ('Aéreo') value through this URL, even though that value, and the associated airline-name/flight-number fields, still exist in the underlying catalog and DOM. See VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Datos de ingreso — Vía de internación* (English: Entry Information — Means of entry*)

fields.name: pointOfEntry
fields.label: Point of entry
fields.description: The specific land border crossing point. Official Spanish names as published in INM's own point-of-entry catalog for land entry (60 values); the English UI renders these same Spanish place names untranslated, since they are proper nouns.
fields.sourceRef: Datos de ingreso — Punto de internación* (English: Entry Information — Point of entry*)

enum: Conexión peatonal aeroportuario Tijuana - San Diego
Puente Internacional I Puerta De México
Punto de Internación Puerta Este en la Puerta México de Tijuana

fields.name: arrivalDate
fields.label: Date of arrival to Mexico
fields.description: The live form displays this as a masked dd/mm/yyyy text input; represent as an ISO 8601 full-date. The live wizard's own client-side validation rejects an arrival date earlier than today or later than 29 days from today (i.e. the wizard may only be completed up to 30 days before arrival, inclusive of today).
fields.sourceRef: Datos de ingreso — Fecha de llegada a México*

fields.name: departureDate
fields.label: Date of departure
fields.description: The live form displays this as a masked dd/mm/yyyy text input; represent as an ISO 8601 full-date. Must not be earlier than arrivalDate, and the live wizard's own client-side validation additionally rejects a departure date more than 179 days after arrivalDate (i.e. a maximum 180-day stay counting the arrival day) — only the first (chronological ordering) half of this rule is expressed in this document's crossFieldValidation; the 180-day cap has no offset-arithmetic operator in the GovSchema Condition grammar and is recorded here as prose instead.
fields.sourceRef: Datos de ingreso — Fecha de salida*

fields.name: givenNames
fields.label: Name(s)
fields.description: As shown on the travel document used to enter Mexico. The live form applies an ICAO-style name pattern (letters plus space/hyphen/apostrophe/period/comma separators, no digits) and converts to upper case.
fields.sourceRef: Datos personales — Nombre(s)*

fields.name: surname
fields.label: Surname(s)
fields.description: As shown on the travel document used to enter Mexico. Same ICAO-style name pattern as givenNames.
fields.sourceRef: Datos personales — Apellido(s)*

fields.name: gender
fields.label: Gender
fields.sourceRef: Datos personales — Sexo* (Hombre/Mujer)

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of birth
fields.description: The live form displays this as a masked dd/mm/yyyy text input; represent as an ISO 8601 full-date. The live wizard's own client-side validation rejects a birth date more than ~110 years (40,177 days) before today, or later than today. The wizard also derives the applicant's age at arrivalDate from this field to (a) decide whether the guardian section (see applicantIsMinor) is required, and (b) remove 'Business' from reasonOfTrip's options when the applicant will be under 2 years old at arrival — neither derivation is itself a field on the live form; see applicantIsMinor and reasonOfTrip.
fields.sourceRef: Datos personales — Fecha de nacimiento*

fields.name: nationality
fields.label: Nationality (Country)
fields.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Drawn from INM's own closed country catalog (243 entries — the same catalog used for countryOfBirth/documentIssuingCountry/countryOfResidence, minus Mexico, which this specific field's option list excludes since the FMM is only completed by non-Mexican-national visitors). Not enumerated here given its size; see VERIFICATION.md for how the live catalog's opaque internal numeric IDs relate to this ISO code.
fields.sourceRef: Datos personales — Nacionalidad (País)*

fields.name: countryOfBirth
fields.label: Country of birth
fields.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Drawn from INM's full 244-entry country catalog (includes Mexico, unlike nationality — a person born in Mexico can hold only a foreign nationality and still use this form). Not enumerated here given its size; see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Datos personales — País de nacimiento*

fields.name: documentType
fields.label: Type of document
fields.sourceRef: Documento de identificación — Tipo de documento* (Pasaporte/Tarjeta pasaporte)

fields.name: documentNumber
fields.label: Document number
fields.sourceRef: Documento de identificación — Número de documento*

fields.name: documentNumberConfirmation
fields.label: Document number (Confirmation)
fields.description: Must exactly match documentNumber. The live form disables copy/paste into this field to force the applicant to retype it.
fields.sourceRef: Documento de identificación — Número de documento (Confirmación)*

fields.name: documentIssuingCountry
fields.label: Country of issue
fields.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Same 244-entry country catalog as countryOfBirth. Not enumerated here given its size; see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Documento de identificación — País de expedición*

fields.name: documentIssueDate
fields.label: Date of issue
fields.description: The live form displays this as a masked dd/mm/yyyy text input; represent as an ISO 8601 full-date. The live wizard's own client-side validation rejects an issue date earlier than dateOfBirth.
fields.sourceRef: Documento de identificación — Fecha de expedición*

fields.name: documentIssueDateConfirmation
fields.label: Date of issue (Confirmation)
fields.description: Must exactly match documentIssueDate. The live form disables copy/paste into this field.
fields.sourceRef: Documento de identificación — Fecha de expedición (Confirmación)*

fields.name: documentExpiryDate
fields.label: Expiration date
fields.description: The live form displays this as a masked dd/mm/yyyy text input; represent as an ISO 8601 full-date. The live wizard's own client-side validation rejects an expiry date earlier than today, and additionally requires it to be at least 179 days after arrivalDate (i.e. the travel document must remain valid through the maximum possible 180-day stay) — the latter offset rule is recorded here as prose only; see departureDate for the same offset-arithmetic limitation.
fields.sourceRef: Documento de identificación — Fecha de expiración*

fields.name: documentExpiryDateConfirmation
fields.label: Expiration date (Confirmation)
fields.description: Must exactly match documentExpiryDate. The live form disables copy/paste into this field.
fields.sourceRef: Documento de identificación — Fecha de expiración (Confirmación)*

fields.name: countryOfResidence
fields.label: Country of residence
fields.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Same 244-entry country catalog as countryOfBirth. Not enumerated here given its size; see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Lugar de residencia — País de residencia*

fields.name: residenceAddress
fields.label: Address of residence
fields.description: Free text; the live form restricts this to upper-case letters, digits, and a small punctuation set ( . , " ' and hyphen), and converts to upper case.
fields.sourceRef: Lugar de residencia — Dirección de residencia*

fields.name: reasonOfTrip
fields.label: Reason of trip
fields.description: Top-level reason category. The live catalog also defines a fourth top-level value, 'Crew' ('Tripulación'), but the wizard's own script removes it from this field's option list whenever means of entry is land (utils.filtraCatalogosPorClave / motivoEstanciaSinTripulacion) — since this document is scoped to land entry, 'crew' is never a reachable value here and is omitted. The wizard additionally removes 'Business' from the option list whenever the applicant will be under 2 years old at arrivalDate; this document does not encode that narrower, age-conditional exclusion (see dateOfBirth), since GovSchema's flat field/Condition model has no mechanism for conditionally shrinking an enum's value set based on another field.
fields.sourceRef: Información de viaje — Motivo de viaje* (Turismo/Negocios/Otro, Tripulación excluded for land entry)

fields.name: reasonOfTripSpecify
fields.label: Specify
fields.description: The specific sub-reason. On the live form the option list populates dynamically based on reasonOfTrip (9 business sub-reasons, 5 other sub-reasons, 1 tourism sub-reason — 'crew' sub-reasons are excluded here for the same land-entry reason as reasonOfTrip); this field is unconditionally required regardless of which reasonOfTrip value is chosen, since every reachable top-level reason has at least one sub-reason. 'resident' has no official English translation on the live source (the English UI falls back to the Spanish label 'Residente'); 'artist_and_sportsman' preserves the live source's own English translation text verbatim, including its own apparent spelling ('Sportman' rather than 'Sportsman').
fields.sourceRef: Información de viaje — Especifique motivo*

fields.name: destinationState
fields.label: State
fields.description: The Mexican state of destination. Official Spanish state names as published in INM's own catalog (32 entries, including Ciudad de México and Estado de México as distinct entries); the English UI renders these same Spanish names untranslated.
fields.sourceRef: Información de viaje — Estado*

fields.name: addressInMexico
fields.label: Address in Mexico
fields.description: Free text (e.g. hotel name, street, and number). Same upper-case letters/digits/limited-punctuation restriction as residenceAddress.
fields.sourceRef: Información de viaje — Domicilio en México* (placeholder: 'Hotel name, street and number')

fields.name: applicantIsMinor
fields.label: Applicant is under 18 years old at arrival
fields.description: Derived field, not itself present on the live form: the wizard computes the applicant's age from dateOfBirth as of arrivalDate and shows/requires the guardian section below whenever that computed age is under 18. GovSchema's flat field/Condition model has no built-in date-arithmetic operator, so this document introduces this boolean as the explicit gate the live computation performs, following the same pattern the spec's own worked example uses for a plain yes/no gate (SPEC.md §6.7's isLLC). Set it to true only when the applicant will genuinely be under 18 on arrivalDate. See VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Derived from Datos personales — Fecha de nacimiento* (not itself a form field; the wizard's utils.validaMenorEdad() function performs this computation client-side)

fields.name: guardianGivenNames
fields.label: Guardian name(s)
fields.description: Name(s) of the accompanying father, mother, or legal guardian. Required only when applicantIsMinor is true.
fields.sourceRef: Información del padre, madre o tutor — Nombre(s)

fields.name: guardianSurname
fields.label: Guardian surname(s)
fields.description: Required only when applicantIsMinor is true.
fields.sourceRef: Información del padre, madre o tutor — Apellido(s)

fields.name: guardianGender
fields.label: Guardian gender
fields.description: Required only when applicantIsMinor is true.
fields.sourceRef: Información del padre, madre o tutor — Sexo

fields.name: guardianDateOfBirth
fields.label: Guardian date of birth
fields.description: Required only when applicantIsMinor is true. The live wizard separately requires the guardian to be an adult (its own age computation on this same field, mirroring dateOfBirth/applicantIsMinor); not re-derived as a second synthetic field here — see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Información del padre, madre o tutor — Fecha de nacimiento

fields.name: guardianNationality
fields.label: Guardian nationality (Country)
fields.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, same 244-entry catalog as countryOfBirth. Required only when applicantIsMinor is true.
fields.sourceRef: Información del padre, madre o tutor — Nacionalidad (País)

fields.name: guardianCountryOfBirth
fields.label: Guardian country of birth
fields.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, same 244-entry catalog as countryOfBirth. Required only when applicantIsMinor is true.
fields.sourceRef: Información del padre, madre o tutor — País de nacimiento

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email
fields.description: Used to send the folio/confirmation and, on final submission, a verification code. The live form converts input to lower case.
fields.sourceRef: Correo electrónico — Correo electrónico*

fields.name: emailConfirmation
fields.label: Email (Confirmation)
fields.description: Must exactly match email. The live form disables copy/paste into this field.
fields.sourceRef: Correo electrónico — Correo electrónico (Confirmación)*
